normalized_title: Development Control Policy 1.8 Canal Estates and Artificial Waterway Developments
scope_summary: Sets out the WAPC's requirements for the assessment of proposals for canal estates, artificial waterways, boat harbours and marinas anywhere in Western Australia. It covers preliminary consultation with State agencies, the approvals pathway (region and local planning schemes, environmental assessment, subdivision, deeds of agreement), design guidelines for waterway dimensions, canal walls, navigation safety, flooding and public open space, and water quality management. Appendix 2 contains model local planning scheme (Canal Zone) provisions including dwelling setbacks and fence/retaining wall height controls for canal-front lots.

key_numeric_standards.topic: minimum depth of clear fairway and mooring areas below Chart Datum
minimum width across water between property boundaries
design flood/erosion event for retaining structures and breakwaters
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: all artificial waterway land retaining structures and breakwaters

key_numeric_standards.topic: public open space contribution (waterways may be partly credited)
minimum dwelling setback from artificial waterway frontage and from road frontage
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: dwellings in Canal Zone (model scheme provisions)

key_numeric_standards.topic: no retaining wall or fencing along waterway frontage / within buffer of waterway wall
maximum retaining wall height within 7.5 m of waterway frontage
maximum retaining wall height between 7.5 m and 9 m from waterway frontage
maximum fence height within 7.5 m of waterway frontage
maximum fence height more than 7.5 m from waterway frontage

cross_references.cited: Development Control Policy 1.8 Canal Estates and Other Artificial Waterway Developments (February 1999) (superseded)
Swan and Canning Rivers Management Act 2006
AS 3962-2001 Guidelines for design of marinas
AS 4997-2005 Guidelines for the design of maritime structures
